titlestmt.titleproper: Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave collection8138

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-06-16 00:15:13 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave collection
did.abstract: The Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave Collection contains posters, flyers, zines, magazines, stickers, catalogs, correspondence, interviews, newspaper and magazine clippings, photos, and other ephemera collected by Tracy Dodgson (1962-2006), the founder of Zero Archives (1997), documenting Punk and New Wave Music and Culture from 1975 to 2007.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave Collection documents the Punk and New Wave Music Scene and Culture mainly in the US, dating from 1975 to 2007, through Zero Archives, a non-profit organization founded by Tracy Dodgson (1962-2006) in 1997. The collection contains posters, flyers, zines, magazines, stickers, catalogs, correspondence, interviews, newspaper and magazine clippings, and other ephemera. There are also a few photos from various artists, programs, and album cover photocopies.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Tracy Dodgson (1962-2006) was born in Canandaigua, NY, and lived most of her life in the Rochester area, NY. In 1997, she founded Zero Archives, a non-profit organization aspiring to focus on collecting, preserving, and disseminating Punk Rock Music History, Lifestyle, and Culture. Dodgson defined Punk Rock music in the broadest sense, including sub-genres like punk, new wave, industrial, Goth, Ska (1977 punk style), and otherwise termed New Music and Alternative Undergrown. She gradually developed this Archive through donations and purchases and actively sought out new and unique bands to document. During the 1980s she hosted a regular country/western music radio program to get hands-on experience in the radio industry. She worked the overnight shift and learned all aspects of program production. It was during this period that she began to buy and sell records, creating her own business that continued for most of her life. Interviewing band members was her favorite activity, along with collecting posters, zines, and other punk movement memorabilia. Tracy was the great granddaughter of the engineer Mr. Frank LaMonte Dodgson, who graduated from Cornell University in 1889 and made contributions to the railroad industry.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave collection, #8138. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 1 and 2 closed for digitization project.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Captain Sensible; The Loudmouths with a signed flyer; inscriptions and other publicity material.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Zero Archive's inventory.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cassettes, Discs, CD-Singles, Albums, Postcards. various record companies: Chainsaw records catalog, Alternatives Tentacles records catalog, Daemon Records catalog, and more. List of Bands.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Zero Archives Inventories.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten interview answers by Jay Heycode. Other artists: Jeff Magnum (Dead Boys), Tucko- California, Antie Christ, Murder, Buzz Clic. Includes correspondence.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: U.S. of A.N.T.S. membership information. Club Devo Fan Club. Awesome Records, Reachout International Records, Empty Records, and more.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images of Olga De Volga (OFFS, Versus, The LEWD), Patti Smith, Johnny Thunders, Wayne Kramer, David Kramer.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Wendy O. Williams (Plasmatics Singer) in a 1981 performance and later.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers by Golden Voice Records; Photocopied duplicates; Included bands are PiL, The LEWD, Originals, Psycho, Dead Kennedys, Versus, The Cramps, Libertine, and more. Concerts in San Francisco (CA), Albany (NY).

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many prints are donated by Glen Tucher/ Aka Tucko CA and others by Olga de Volga. Most of the material is in photocopies. Included bands are Dead Kennedy, VTKTMS, Cathay de Grande, Comateens, Sayyadina, Cyness, Nip Drivers, The Bongos, VerSus, The Avengers, The Offs, Whisky, Sleepers, Adolescents, The Black Jack, Screamers, Scorgies, Rockats, the Farms, Cramps Blasters and more.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posters from concerts mainly by VS. (Versus) and Off. Included bands: Crime, Untouchables, Avengers, Mutants, The Nuns, SSI, Jars, Sudden Fun, Benefit, and more. Some posters are in copies. Some posters are donated by Olga de Volga (see Box 1, Folder 8). See relevant material in Mabuhay Folder, Box 1 Folder 20.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artists: N.O.T.A., Rest in Pieces, Misfits, Ramones, Freakazoid, VooDooand, and more. Concerts in San Francisco CA, Albany NY, Kansas City KS, Clarkston, MI, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Punk Rock Movie from England by Don Letts Poster, Crime, Johnny Rotten, and the Sex Pistols, The Slits, posters with Olga de Volga (see also relevant material Box 1 Folder 8), The Lewd, Circle Jerks, VS, SSI, Comateens, Suicidal Tendencies, The Red Hot Chilli Peppers, Weirdos and more.

did.unittitle: Series IV. Album Covers, Programs and Stickers

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Police Image Limited Tour in Europe (1983); Tour Programs; Avant-Garde Music/ Art Festival (1983); The Loudmouths European Tour 1999 sticker; Dead Kennedy sticker; Dr. Strange Records stickers

did.unittitle: Series V. Clippings from Newspapers and Magazines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles, newspaper-printed photos, and ads referred to Black Flag, X band, Elvis Costello, Phil Starling, Andy Warhol, Nico, John Cale, Lou Reed, Sex Pistols, Patti Smith, Mastodon, David Bowie, the New York Dolls, and more. Includes newspapers such as Berkeley Independent, Enquirer Newspaper, Fuck Facts!, Hangover from a plastic feast, and Gazette.

did.unittitle: Series VI. Band or Nightclub Publicity Material

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers donated by Glen Tucher/ Aka Tucko CA. Most of the material is in photocopies.

container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers donated by Glen Tucher/ Aka Tucko CA. Most of the material is in photocopies.

container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers donated by Steve Seitz. Most of the material is in photocopies.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers donated by Olga De Volga, Onetime DKS Manager Chi-Chi (Barbara). Most of the material is in photocopies.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers donated by Gientucker, So California, the Loudmouths. Most of the material is in photocopies.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers donated by Glen Tucher/ Aka Tucko CA; Artists: The Offs, Crime, Cathay de Grande, and more. Most of the material is in photocopies.

container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070587443]

did.unittitle: Punk Rock…100 nights at the Roxy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early UK punk club, UK issue original; Some pages out, but Scattered thru archives; Classic Photos.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Central New York's alternative music rag. No 2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Talk of Hollywood; Vol.2, No 31 (Hollywood, 1984). L.A. Zine. Dodgson bought in Hollywood on Melrose Ave. T. Zero.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue 1, Sept. Oct. 1984; Issue 2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue 24, Winter 97/98.

did.unittitle: The bible: UK 1976-1977, Page collection Sniffin' Glue Fanzine by Michael Dempsey PUNK

scopecontent.p: Issue 15.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richard Gossett plays the Sex Pistols, The Damned, the Screamers; Interview with Ritchie Detrick, and the Crime.

scopecontent.p: Issue 2, Vol.I (Albany, NY, 1986)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue 9. Autographed by Plasmatics Singer Wendy o Williams to the Guy from Bad religion.

did.unittitle: 1998: The New Wave Punk Rock Explosion by Caroline Coon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interviews with S.B.'s RKL, Spattle's, The accused, Atlanta's Neon Christ plus a special punk editorial section, photos, scene reports from Arizona, Kansas City, Boston. Montreal and record reviews. False Confessions meet AC/DC.
